question.text: In what scenario would a higher tick size be more beneficial for algo traders compared to hedgers?

answer.text: Higher tick size benefits algo traders as the minimum price movement generates larger P&L per tick
Higher tick size benefits hedgers as they can get more precise hedging
Tick size has no impact on either category
Lower tick size benefits algo traders more than higher tick size

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Higher tick size benefits algo traders as each minimum price movement generates a larger P&L (tick value). Lower tick size benefits hedgers as they can achieve more precise price matching for their hedge. **The Trap:** Higher tick size makes hedging LESS precise. The workbook explicitly states "higher tick size benefits algo traders/speculators while lower tick size benefits hedgers." **Cross-Exam Context:** Tick size considerations are specific to commodity derivatives trading mechanics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Different commodity contracts have different tick sizes — traders should choose contracts with tick sizes that suit their strategy (precise hedging vs tick-by-tick trading).
